title: Create TValueGenerator typedef and TDummySchema sealed tree
blocks: All later tasks that depend on a schema tree or a generator callback signature (registry, probing map, dummy api skeleton).

status_instructions.on_followup: If you discovered a naming or structural nuance the registry/probing tasks must honour, append to their `side_context`.

description: Introduce the generators/ concept folder under lib/ and create the two smallest but foundational types: the `TValueGenerator` callback typedef and the recursive `TDummySchema` tree with a sealed `TDummySchemaField` hierarchy (leaf for flat resolved types, branch for nested DTOs). These types are the shared vocabulary every other task in this feature will depend on.
context_for_junior: This package currently has no generators/ folder. The spec introduces it as a new 'concept folder' that hosts the fake-data generator system for the dummy Firestore api. A typedef in Dart is just a named alias for a function signature — think of it as a single shorthand for `dynamic Function()` so every downstream file can say `TValueGenerator` instead of rewriting the signature. The schema type is the output of a probing phase that happens later: it describes 'for this entity type, field `name` resolved to String, field `nested` resolved to another nested schema, and field `customThing` could not be resolved'. We need the container type now because the probing map (later task), the registry (later task), and the dummy api itself all take or return a schema.
why_it_matters: Every other feature task reads from or writes to these two types. Getting names, shapes, and visibility right now prevents a cascade of painful downstream edits.
instructions: Create lib/generators/ if it does not exist. Respect one-type-per-file from dev-conventions/conventions/folders-and-file-types.md.

Create lib/generators/t_value_generator.dart containing ONLY `typedef TValueGenerator = dynamic Function();` and a short doc comment explaining it is the callback contract every value provider implements.

Create lib/generators/t_dummy_schema.dart containing: the sealed class `TDummySchemaField` with two final subclasses `TDummySchemaLeaf` and `TDummySchemaBranch`, plus the `TDummySchema` data class with `Map<String, TDummySchemaField> fields` and `Set<String> unresolved`.
TDummySchemaLeaf carries `Type type`. TDummySchemaBranch carries `TDummySchema nested`.
Both the typedef file and the schema file must be safe to export publicly.
Add short dartdoc on every public declaration: one sentence on purpose, one sentence on when the consumer will encounter it.
Run `make analyze package=turbo_firestore_api`. Must pass with 0 warnings.
goals: Two new files that compile, lint-clean, and are ready to be imported by the registry + probing map + dummy api.
Recursive schema tree supports nested DTOs out of the box (a branch node contains another full TDummySchema).
Sealed hierarchy gives later code exhaustive pattern matching over leaf vs branch.
non_goals: No public exports from turbo_firestore_api.dart yet — that is task 11.
No equality/hashCode/copyWith helpers unless strictly required by analyzer.
acceptance_criteria: `lib/generators/t_value_generator.dart` exists, analyzes clean, contains the typedef and nothing else.

Unit test covering: (a) constructing a flat schema with two leaf fields, (b) constructing a nested schema branch, (c) verifying the sealed hierarchy exhaustively pattern-matches via a `switch` expression.
constraints: One public type per file — do not co-locate TValueGenerator and TDummySchema.
No external dependencies added to pubspec.yaml.
Trailing commas, single quotes, const where possible.
tools: RepoPrompt MCP for reading existing lib/ layout

testing.rules: Follow TestRulesAndReasons and dev-conventions/conventions/unit-tests.md. Test outcomes and state, not implementation details. No mocks — these types have no collaborators.
scenarios: Given a flat schema with two leaf fields, When fields are inspected, Then each field reports the expected Type.
Given a schema with one branch field, When the branch is destructured, Then the nested TDummySchema is accessible and carries its own fields.
Given a sealed TDummySchemaField value, When pattern-matched in a switch expression, Then the Dart analyzer reports exhaustiveness with no default required.
testing.refactor_for_testability: If the schema constructor ends up hard to test, split into pure factory constructors per variant rather than reaching for mocks.

quality_bar.maintainability: Sealed hierarchy keeps future additions (e.g. TDummySchemaListItem) explicit and forces downstream pattern matches to update.
quality_bar.dry: The typedef lives once and is referenced everywhere downstream.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: Schema describes shape, nothing else — no generation logic leaks into it.
